nodes.description: Regulatory label claim asserting product meets gluten-free standard (<20 ppm)

nodes.description: Production lot of Chocolate Bar Classic manufactured at Confectionery Line C

nodes.label: Allergen Cross-Contact Check
nodes.description: Before a production run, verify cleaning protocol was completed and validated between allergen and allergen-free products.

nodes.label: Recall Scoping Workflow
nodes.description: When an allergen contamination is detected, trace affected lots, production runs, and distribution endpoints with precision.

nodes.label: Reformulation Approval Process
nodes.description: When reformulating for allergen removal, validate new ingredients, update labels, test for cross-reactivity, and obtain regulatory clearance.

nodes.label: Line Changeover Cleaning Validation
nodes.description: Taught by Food Safety Manager: After any tree nut product run, the allergen swab test must show < 5 ppm before starting a nut-free product. Standard says 10 ppm but we had a near-miss at 8 ppm.
nodes.creator: people-food-safety-mgr

nodes.label: Supplier Allergen Cert Expiry Alert
nodes.description: Observed that 3 supplier allergen certifications expired without notice in 2024. System now alerts 60 days before expiry.
nodes.creator: people-food-safety-mgr

nodes.label: Label Allergen Cross-Check
nodes.description: Mined from 50 label reviews: 12% of label updates after reformulation miss updating the 'may contain' advisory statement.
nodes.creator: people-rd-lead

nodes.label: Food Safety Manager
nodes.description: Department level. Manages food safety protocols, allergen controls, and HACCP compliance.

nodes.label: R&D Lead
nodes.description: Individual level. Leads product development, reformulations, and label compliance.

nodes.label: Food Safety
nodes.description: Manages food safety standards, allergen controls, HACCP, and recall procedures.

nodes.label: Product Development
nodes.description: Develops new products, manages reformulations, and maintains recipe specifications.

questions.question: Soy Lecithin from Supplier A found to contain undeclared milk protein. What is the exposure?
questions.answer: Direct exposure: 3 products using Soy Lecithin across 2 plants. But the Changeover Cleaning skill catches a 4th product at risk - Angel Food Cake Mix was produced after Chocolate Truffle Bar with a 7 ppm swab result (above our 5 ppm standard). The Label Cross-Check skill flags Protein Energy Bites as labeled 'dairy-free' - requires immediate hold. Total recall scope: 4 products, 3 retail endpoints. Food Safety Manager gets full scope. R&D Lead corrects labels.
reasoningSteps: Recall Scoping Workflow triggers on allergen contamination.
Entity traversal: Soy Lecithin -> used in affected products.
Lot tracing: affected batches -> production lots at plants.
Distribution tracing: affected lots -> retail endpoints.
Skill fires: "Line Changeover Cleaning Validation" - checks production logs for cross-contact risk on adjacent product runs.
Skill fires: "Label Allergen Cross-Check" - checks if any affected products are labeled dairy-free incorrectly.
Allergen Cross-Contact Check: adjacent product from same line is now suspect. Add to recall scope.
People routed: Food Safety Manager gets recall scope. R&D Lead gets label correction priority list.

questions.question: If Soy Lecithin is found to contain undeclared milk protein, which products, lots, and stores are affected?
questions.answer: Soy Lecithin is used in Chocolate Bar Classic via its recipe. Since it now contains an undeclared dairy allergen, Chocolate Bar Classic is impacted. Batch 101 produced at Confectionery Line C and distributed to Grocery Chain A is affected. A targeted recall can be issued for this specific lot instead of all chocolate products.
reasoningSteps: Soy Lecithin is used in Chocolate Bar Recipe
Chocolate Bar Recipe produces Chocolate Bar Classic
Chocolate Bar Classic is produced as Batch 101
Batch 101 is manufactured at Confectionery Line C
Batch 101 is distributed to Grocery Chain A
Undeclared dairy allergen triggers targeted recall for this lot

questions.question: Which production lines process both allergen and non-allergen products, and are cleaning protocols validated?
questions.answer: Bakery Line A processes gluten, peanut, and egg-containing ingredients across multiple products including Gluten-Free Bread. This creates cross-contamination risk. While a gluten cleaning protocol is validated, the nut cleaning protocol is not validated, indicating a compliance gap for peanut allergen removal.
reasoningSteps: Bakery Line A handles Wheat Flour (gluten), Peanut Oil (peanut), Egg Albumin (egg)
Same line produces Gluten-Free Bread
Cross-contact risk exists between allergen and non-allergen products
Gluten cleaning protocol is validated
Nut cleaning protocol is not validated
This creates compliance risk for allergen removal

questions.question: We want to ensure Angel Food Cake Mix is gluten-free. What needs to change?
questions.answer: Angel Food Cake Mix currently contains Wheat Flour, which introduces gluten. To make it gluten-free, Wheat Flour must be replaced with Rice Flour. Flour Mill Co supplies Rice Flour and holds gluten-free certification, making it a viable alternative supplier for reformulation.
reasoningSteps: Angel Food Cake Recipe uses Wheat Flour
Wheat Flour contains gluten allergen
To remove gluten, Wheat Flour must be replaced
Rice Flour is a gluten-free alternative
Flour Mill Co supplies Rice Flour
Supplier has gluten-free certification
Reformulation is feasible with existing supplier

questions.question: Are there any products labeled gluten-free that still have allergen risks?
questions.answer: Gluten-Free Bread is labeled as gluten-free and uses Rice Flour. However, it is produced on Bakery Line A, which also processes Wheat Flour. Although a gluten cleaning protocol is validated, shared equipment introduces residual cross-contact risk that must be continuously monitored and verified.
reasoningSteps: Gluten-Free Bread carries gluten-free label
It uses Rice Flour (no gluten)
It is produced on Bakery Line A
Bakery Line A also processes Wheat Flour
Gluten cleaning protocol is validated
Shared equipment still introduces residual risk
Continuous validation is required for compliance

questions.question: Which products are affected if Dairy Processor Midwest has a contamination issue?
questions.answer: Dairy Processor Midwest supplies Milk Powder and Whey Protein Isolate. These ingredients are used in Chocolate Bar Classic and Protein Shake Vanilla. Both products would be impacted across all production and distribution channels, requiring quality review and potential recall.
reasoningSteps: Supplier provides Milk Powder and Whey Protein Isolate
Milk Powder is used in Chocolate Bar Recipe
Chocolate Bar Recipe produces Chocolate Bar Classic
Whey Protein Isolate is used in Protein Shake Recipe
Protein Shake Recipe produces Protein Shake Vanilla
Both products depend on this supplier
Contamination affects both product lines across multiple stores
